meta.title: SF-SPAP-1: Platform Fundamentals, Loading and Querying

questions.stem: Which Snowflake layer stores table data in compressed columnar files?
questions.explanation: The storage layer persists Snowflake data, while Cloud Services manages metadata and warehouses execute queries.

questions.stem: What is a virtual warehouse used for in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: Virtual warehouses provide compute for query execution; storage, security, and marketplace features are separate concerns.

questions.stem: Which statement about Snowflake compute and storage is correct?

options.text: Adding users requires a larger warehouse
Data sharing copies table data into each consumer account

questions.explanation: Snowflake decouples compute from storage, so warehouses and stored data scale separately.

questions.stem: Which object hierarchy is valid in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: Organizations contain accounts, and accounts contain databases, schemas, and data objects.

questions.stem: Which interface is specifically a command-line client for Snowflake?
questions.explanation: SnowSQL is the CLI; Snowsight is the browser UI, Snowpipe is ingestion, and Snowpark is a developer API.

questions.stem: A team needs 90 days of Time Travel for selected tables. Which edition requirement matters most?

options.text: Enterprise Edition or higher is required
Business Critical is the only option

questions.explanation: Extended Time Travel retention beyond the default short window requires higher editions such as Enterprise or above.

questions.stem: Which activities are handled by the Cloud Services layer? (Select 2.)

options.text: Persisting table files in object storage

questions.explanation: Cloud Services handles metadata, optimization, and access coordination. Storage persists files, while warehouses execute query operators.

questions.stem: What does auto-resume on a warehouse do?

options.text: Drops cached query results after inactivity
Starts compute automatically when a query needs it
Replicates warehouse settings to other accounts
Converts a single-cluster warehouse into multi-cluster

questions.explanation: Auto-resume restarts suspended compute when work arrives; it does not change topology or replication behavior.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake object is best for organizing related tables and views within a database?
questions.explanation: Schemas group data objects inside a database. Warehouses provide compute, roles grant privileges, and pipes automate ingestion.

questions.stem: Why can two teams run large queries at the same time without one warehouse blocking the other?

options.text: Because all queries run in Cloud Services
Because each team can use a separate virtual warehouse against shared storage
Because Snowflake duplicates the database for each user
Because every table has its own built-in warehouse

questions.explanation: Independent warehouses can query the same shared storage concurrently without copying the data.

questions.stem: Which statement best describes micro-partitions?

options.text: Users must define them manually before loading data
They are automatically maintained units of columnar storage metadata and pruning
They are identical to traditional database partitions
They only exist for temporary tables

questions.explanation: Snowflake automatically creates and manages micro-partitions and uses their metadata for pruning.

questions.stem: Which client experience is Snowflake’s web-based user interface?
questions.explanation: Snowsight is the web UI for worksheets, dashboards, and administration tasks.

questions.stem: Which stage is scoped to a single table by default?
questions.explanation: A table stage is tied to a single table, while user and named stages have broader scope.

questions.stem: Which command is used for bulk loading files into a table?
questions.explanation: COPY INTO <table> is the core bulk load command. PUT uploads files to an internal stage, and GET downloads from a stage.

questions.stem: What is a primary benefit of Snowpipe compared with scheduled bulk COPY jobs?
questions.explanation: Snowpipe is designed for near-continuous loading when files arrive, unlike manually scheduled batch COPY processes.

questions.stem: Which file formats can Snowflake load natively without converting files to CSV first? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Snowflake natively supports semi-structured formats such as Parquet and JSON. Executables and slides are not supported data formats.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: supported data file formats

questions.stem: A team wants a table with no Fail-safe and lower recovery cost for transient staging data. Which table type fits best?
questions.explanation: Transient tables skip Fail-safe and are commonly used for staging data where reduced data protection is acceptable.

questions.stem: Which COPY option helps keep loading when a few records are malformed?
questions.explanation: ON_ERROR controls how COPY reacts to bad rows or files. The other options do not govern row-level load errors.

questions.stem: What does the PUT command do in Snowflake?

options.text: Uploads local files to an internal stage

questions.explanation: PUT uploads files into an internal stage so they can be loaded later.

questions.stem: Which data type is commonly used to store semi-structured JSON in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: VARIANT stores semi-structured values such as JSON, Avro, ORC, XML, and Parquet content.

questions.stem: Why might a team choose an external stage instead of an internal stage?

options.text: To store files in cloud storage they already manage
Because COPY INTO cannot read internal stages
Because external stages are required for temporary tables

questions.explanation: External stages point at customer-managed cloud storage such as S3, Azure Blob, or GCS.

questions.stem: Which statement about temporary tables is correct?

options.text: They are visible to all users in an account
They persist until manually dropped across sessions
They exist only for the session that created them

questions.explanation: Temporary tables are session-scoped and disappear at the end of the session.

questions.stem: What does PURGE=TRUE in COPY INTO usually indicate?

options.text: Remove successfully loaded files from the stage after loading
Disable Time Travel for the table

questions.explanation: PURGE can remove stage files after successful load, reducing stage clutter.

questions.stem: Which clause filters grouped results after aggregation?
questions.explanation: HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY aggregation. WHERE filters rows before aggregation.

questions.stem: Which join returns only rows with matching keys in both tables?
questions.explanation: INNER JOIN keeps only matches that appear in both inputs.

questions.stem: How do you access the city field inside a VARIANT column named payload?
questions.explanation: Snowflake commonly uses colon or bracket notation such as payload:city to traverse VARIANT values.

questions.stem: Which functions are aggregate functions in SQL? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: COUNT and SUM aggregate across rows. COALESCE and SUBSTR are scalar functions.

questions.stem: Which clause is most appropriate for sorting query output from highest revenue to lowest revenue?
questions.explanation: ORDER BY controls presentation order of the final result set.

questions.stem: Which result is produced by a LEFT JOIN when a left-side row has no match on the right?

options.text: The row is returned with NULLs for right-side columns
The row is duplicated once per warehouse cluster

questions.explanation: LEFT JOIN preserves left-side rows and fills unmatched right-side columns with NULL.

questions.stem: Which expression is best for returning only the date part of a timestamp?
questions.explanation: TO_DATE converts or extracts the date value from a timestamp expression.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: date and time functions

questions.stem: A query groups by region and calculates AVG(sales). Which clause determines the grouping key?
questions.explanation: GROUP BY defines how rows are grouped before aggregate functions are computed.

questions.stem: Which function converts a string containing JSON into a semi-structured value?
questions.explanation: PARSE_JSON turns a JSON string into a VARIANT value for downstream querying.

questions.stem: What does FLATTEN help do with semi-structured data?

options.text: Expand arrays or nested structures into rows

questions.explanation: FLATTEN is used to explode arrays or nested objects into relational rows.

questions.stem: Which clause limits a result set to the first 10 rows returned?
questions.explanation: LIMIT restricts the number of rows returned to the client.

questions.stem: When a timestamp is converted between time zones, which Snowflake data concern is most relevant?
questions.explanation: Timezone conversion behavior depends on the timestamp type and date/time functions used.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: date, time, and timestamp data types

questions.stem: Which Snowflake security model is used to grant privileges to users through roles?
questions.explanation: Snowflake uses role-based access control so privileges are typically granted to roles, then roles to users.

questions.stem: Which system role is commonly responsible for managing grants, users, and roles?
questions.explanation: SECURITYADMIN manages users and roles, while SYSADMIN focuses on object creation and operation.

questions.stem: What does MFA primarily add to username/password authentication?
questions.explanation: MFA strengthens authentication by requiring another factor beyond a password.

questions.stem: Which statement about network policies is correct?

options.text: They control SQL syntax allowed in worksheets
They can restrict allowed client IP addresses
They only work for external stages

questions.explanation: Network policies let administrators restrict which IP addresses can access Snowflake.

questions.stem: How is data encrypted when moving between a client and Snowflake?

options.text: It is not encrypted unless Business Critical is enabled

questions.explanation: Snowflake encrypts data in transit with TLS.

questions.stem: Which features provide fine-grained protection of sensitive data values? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Masking can obfuscate column values, while row access policies can filter which rows a user sees.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: masking policies and row access policies

questions.stem: Why is it usually better to grant privileges to a custom role instead of directly to many users?

options.text: It simplifies administration and least-privilege management
It is required for loading JSON

questions.explanation: Roles make privilege administration scalable and support least-privilege design.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: role hierarchy and grants

questions.stem: Which authentication method is commonly used for service users that should avoid storing passwords?
questions.explanation: Key-pair authentication is a standard option for service principals and automation clients.

questions.stem: What is the PUBLIC role in Snowflake?

options.text: A built-in role automatically granted to all users

questions.explanation: PUBLIC is a built-in role present for all users, so administrators should be careful with grants to it.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake capability lets a provider share live data without copying table files into the consumer account?
questions.explanation: Secure data sharing exposes live data objects without copying physical data.

questions.stem: When is a reader account most useful?

options.text: When the consumer already has its own Snowflake account
When a consumer needs shared data but does not have a Snowflake account

questions.explanation: Reader accounts allow providers to share with consumers who are not already Snowflake customers.

questions.stem: What is the Snowflake Marketplace used for?

options.text: Buying warehouse credits from other customers
Discovering and consuming third-party data products and applications

questions.explanation: Marketplace listings are used to distribute data products and applications.

questions.stem: Which object is created by a provider to expose databases, schemas, and secure views to consumers?
questions.explanation: A share is the provider-side object used for secure data sharing.

questions.stem: What is one difference between Data Exchange and Marketplace?

options.text: Data Exchange is for private collaboration groups, while Marketplace is a broader discovery channel
Data Exchange can only share CSV files

questions.explanation: Data Exchange focuses on controlled private collaboration, while Marketplace is a larger distribution surface.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: Data Exchange and Marketplace

questions.stem: Which objects are commonly shared instead of raw base tables to enforce governed access? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Secure views and secure UDFs help govern what consumers can see. Warehouses are not shared as data objects.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: secure objects and data sharing

questions.stem: A provider wants usage visibility for a data product listing. Which Snowflake feature is most relevant?
questions.explanation: Provider analytics on listings help track installs and usage trends.
questions.reference: Snowflake documentation: provider listings and analytics

questions.stem: Which term best describes granting another account access to selected objects while the provider keeps ownership?
questions.explanation: Secure data sharing grants controlled access without transferring ownership or duplicating data.

questions.stem: Why are secure views often preferred in shared datasets?

options.text: They let providers hide underlying table logic and restrict exposed data
They eliminate the need for privileges
They copy source rows to the consumer account

questions.explanation: Secure views preserve governance by exposing only intended columns/rows and hiding implementation details.

questions.stem: What is Snowpark primarily used for?

options.text: Building DataFrame-style applications in languages like Python, Java, and Scala inside Snowflake
Uploading files to stages from a laptop
Replacing all SQL queries in Snowsight

questions.explanation: Snowpark is the developer framework for writing DataFrame and procedural logic that executes in Snowflake.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake capability lets developers build Python-based interactive apps directly in the platform?
questions.explanation: Streamlit in Snowflake hosts interactive Python apps natively.

questions.stem: What is the Native App Framework designed for?

options.text: Creating and distributing applications that run in consumer Snowflake accounts
Replacing role hierarchy with app roles only

questions.explanation: The Native App Framework packages and distributes applications for consumers to install in their own accounts.

questions.stem: Which tool is most directly associated with command-line automation of Snowflake resources and app packaging?
questions.explanation: Snowflake CLI supports packaging, deployment, and management workflows from the command line.

questions.stem: Which tools are examples of partner or adjacent technologies commonly integrated with Snowflake? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: dbt and Tableau are common ecosystem integrations, unlike micro-partitions or worksheets which are internal platform concepts.

questions.stem: Why might a team use Streamlit in Snowflake instead of hosting a separate dashboard server?

options.text: To keep the app close to Snowflake data and simplify deployment
Because it requires no Python code
Because it only works for external consumers

questions.explanation: Streamlit in Snowflake reduces operational overhead and keeps the app close to the governed data platform.

questions.stem: Which statement best distinguishes Snowpark from standard SQL worksheets?

options.text: Snowpark is for programmatic DataFrame and procedural development, while worksheets are an interactive SQL interface
Snowpark is only a security feature
Worksheets are required to load data files

questions.explanation: Snowpark offers developer APIs, whereas worksheets are mainly an interactive query environment.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake layer stores table data in compressed columnar files? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: The storage layer persists Snowflake data, while Cloud Services manages metadata and warehouses execute queries. A second closely related platform concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: What is a virtual warehouse used for in Snowflake? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Virtual warehouses provide compute for query execution; storage, security, and marketplace features are separate concerns. A second closely related platform concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which statement about Snowflake compute and storage is correct? (Select 2.)

options.text: Adding users requires a larger warehouse
Data sharing copies table data into each consumer account

questions.explanation: Snowflake decouples compute from storage, so warehouses and stored data scale separately. A second closely related platform concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which object hierarchy is valid in Snowflake? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: Organizations contain accounts, and accounts contain databases, schemas, and data objects. A second closely related platform concept is also correct in this variant.

questions.stem: Which interface is specifically a command-line client for Snowflake? (Select 2.)
questions.explanation: SnowSQL is the CLI; Snowsight is the browser UI, Snowpipe is ingestion, and Snowpark is a developer API. A second closely related platform concept is also correct in this variant.
